title: Stable engine ABI contract + cross-language golden fixtures
description:
## Background

The engine ABI is the cross-language contract. Every native binding (Node, Python, Java, .NET, Go) and the WebAssembly artifact all consume it. Stability is non-negotiable; once published, the C ABI follows semver.

## Goal

A documented, frozen C ABI for the engine, plus cross-language golden fixtures that test every binding for byte-equivalence.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] C ABI documented in `crates/invoicekit-ffi/ABI.md`.
- [ ] Header file `invoicekit.h` generated and committed.
- [ ] Golden fixtures in `conformance-corpus/golden/` consumed by every binding's test suite.
- [ ] Cross-language CI: each binding (Node, Python, Java, .NET, Go, WASM) runs the golden suite on every PR.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

(No type-specific gates apply to this bead beyond the universal gates above.)

## Implementation notes

Use `cbindgen` to generate the header. Use opaque pointers + canonical-JSON byte streams across the boundary.

## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §2.1 dual delivery shapes; §4.1.
close_reason: Closed by PR https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/22 (merge 750748c) — Engine ABI v1, C FFI ABI.md/header, shared golden fixture, and cross-language CI for Rust wrappers, Python, Bun/Node, Java, .NET, Go, and WASM landed. All PR checks green including engine ABI golden suite. Fresh-eyes AI review returned NO BLOCKERS; notes addressed before merge. Waived gates: none; type-specific gates: N/A.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: define an ABI v1 JSON byte-stream contract in invoicekit-engine, with operations represented by canonical JSON requests and responses. Implement a first deterministic operation that validates/canonicalizes a CommercialDocument JSON payload through the Rust IR and RFC 8785 canonicalization; expose the same contract through invoicekit-ffi using opaque result handles plus generated/committed invoicekit.h and ABI.md. Add conformance-corpus/golden fixtures with exact expected response bytes, then consume that fixture from engine, ffi, wasm, and existing Rust binding crates. For README-only Java/.NET/Go scaffolds, either add honest fixture-checking harnesses or file blocker beads if actual cross-language CI cannot be satisfied without their dedicated binding implementation beads.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Implemented Engine ABI v1 contract and cross-language golden suite. Evidence: cbindgen --verify passes for crates/invoicekit-ffi/include/invoicekit.h; C and C++ header compile smokes pass; cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test --locked -p invoicekit-engine -p invoicekit-ffi -p invoicekit-wasm -p invoicekit-binding-node-napi -p invoicekit-binding-python -p invoicekit-binding-wasm-browser -p invoicekit-binding-rest-shim; Python ctypes, Bun FFI, Go cgo, and Java FFM golden harnesses pass locally; cargo build/test/clippy/doc workspace passed; cargo audit and cargo deny passed; project Python tooling tests passed. .NET harness is wired in CI but not run locally because dotnet is unavailable. Fresh-eyes subagent returned NO BLOCKERS; non-blocking notes were addressed by cpp_compat header generation, strict ABI envelope rejection, and use-after-free documentation.

- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).

- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).

- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.

## Type-specific quality gates

### WebAssembly gates (label `wasm`)
- [ ] WASM artifact size measured and tracked; bundle stays under the budget documented in PLAN.md §2.1 (default 5 MB with feature set `de,fr,it,peppol`).
- [ ] Feature flags compile cleanly: at least 3 distinct feature combinations build and pass tests.
- [ ] Cold-start latency on Cloudflare Workers under the budget in PLAN.md (best-effort; benchmark recorded if exceeded).
- [ ] External-backend calls (`jvm:*`, `cli:*`, `rest:official`, `partner`) return `RequiresExternalBackend` errors with remediation text — they never panic, never silently downgrade.

## Implementation notes

Use `wasm-bindgen`. Output published to npm as `@invoicekit/wasm`.

## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §2.1 feature-flagged WebAssembly builds.

PR admin-merged because both agents share GitHub identity.

title: Validator sidecar protocol + per-domain workers (kosit, phive, saxon)
description:
## Background

Reference validators run as per-domain JVM sidecars: validator-kosit, validator-phive, validator-saxon, plus validator-verapdf (T-052), validator-phase4 (T-092). Not a monolithic JVM. See PLAN.md §2.6, §4.5.

## Goal

Three containerized JVM sidecars (kosit, phive, saxon) speaking a stable JSON-RPC contract.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Each sidecar runs as a separate Docker container.
- [ ] JSON-RPC contract documented in `services/validator-rpc.md`.
- [ ] Latency: validate a 1 MB XML in under 200 ms (p95) on each sidecar.
- [ ] Smoke test: each sidecar produces correct output for a known good and known bad sample.
- [ ] CI builds each container image on every PR.

close_reason: Closed by PR https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/25, merged as 3b38f9b128660e0312f702f0d742b3aa9511f0f8. Shipped shared Java 21 validator sidecar host, separate KoSIT/phive/Saxon Docker images, documented JSON-RPC contract, Docker smoke/latency harness, and CI image build/smoke job. PR CI green including validator sidecar images. Fresh-eyes review: Epicurus NO BLOCKERS. Waived/N/A gates: validator differential parity, per-rule tests, and rule-pack manifest checks are downstream T-031/T-123 rule-engine parity scope; this bead establishes the sidecar protocol/container/JVM oracle dependency boundary. Rust coverage is N/A because primary implementation is Java/Python and is covered by Docker real-service smoke/latency harness.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement T-030 as a shared Java 21 JSON-RPC sidecar host plus three separate Docker images for validator-kosit, validator-phive, and validator-saxon. Each image is built from its own Dockerfile and Maven profile so the backend dependency boundary is explicit: org.kosit:validator:1.6.2, com.helger.phive.rules:phive-rules-peppol:3.2.2, and net.sf.saxon:Saxon-HE:12.9. The host verifies the backend oracle class is present at startup, exposes /healthz and /rpc, returns T-032 ValidationResult-shaped findings for XML well-formedness failures, and records oracle metadata in trace details. Add services/validator-rpc.md for the stable contract and services/validator-smoke.py to start each container, smoke good/bad XML, and enforce p95 <200ms for a 1 MB XML request. CI will build all three images with BuildKit and run the smoke/latency harness. Validator parity/per-rule gates are N/A for this sidecar-protocol bead because these containers are the JVM oracle boundary; T-031/T-123 own Rust-vs-oracle parity.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: implementation and local gates complete for PR prep. UBS reviewed: fixed JSON decode and executor lifecycle warnings; remaining Java hard-coded-secret warnings are false positives on static metadata/hash field names. Fresh-eyes review from Epicurus: NO BLOCKERS; full domain rule invocation explicitly documented as downstream parity scope.

title: EN 16931 hand-written Rust validator (~50 core rules)
description:
## Background

Hand-written Rust implementation of the EN 16931 core business rules (~50 rules). Pure-Rust validation ships with the engine; differential-tested against the JVM sidecars for parity.

## Goal

Pure-Rust validator covers all ~50 EN 16931 core rules at 99.9% parity with the JVM oracle.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Every BR-* and BR-CO-* rule from EN 16931 implemented as a typed Rust function.
- [ ] Differential test against `validator-kosit` and `validator-phive`: 99.9% parity on the conformance corpus.
- [ ] Per-rule unit tests with at least one positive and one negative case each.
- [ ] Performance: validate a 1 MB invoice in under 25 ms (p95) on a baseline reference machine.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### Validator gates (label `validators`)
- [ ] Differential parity vs the named JVM oracle: ≥ 99.9% rule-result match on the conformance corpus. Mismatches are catalogued, classified (oracle bug vs. our bug), and tracked.
- [ ] Per-rule unit tests: at least one positive and one negative case per rule.
- [ ] Each rule includes a `citation` referencing the rule pack source (EN 16931 PDF section, KoSIT XSL, Peppol Schematron, etc.).
- [ ] Rule pack version pinned via T-017 manifest; PR fails if pack manifest is unsigned or stale.

## Implementation notes

Rules go in `crates/validate-ubl-cii/src/rules/`.

## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §4.5 validation.
close_reason: Completed direct on main: EN 16931 Rust validator inventory is 86/86 implemented with focused unit/perf coverage already in place, and the remaining T-031 gate now passes live JVM differential parity against both KoSIT and phive. Evidence: combined tools/validator-parity/en16931_parity.py run compared 50 UBL fixtures per backend with 0 mismatches and parity 1.0 for jvm:kosit and jvm:phive; Dockerized Maven tests pass for -Pkosit and -Pphive; Rust tests/clippy/doc/fmt pass for affected crates. PR gate waived by explicit principal instruction to stop PR/worktree flow and work directly on main via Agent Mail.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Blocked after source check, not implementation-started. Official CEN/ConnectingEurope eInvoicing-EN16931 validation-1.3.16 UBL Schematron exposes 81 distinct BR-* / BR-CO-* ids for the core model, while the bead title says ~50. Current invoicekit-ir cannot represent several required EN16931 business terms needed for positive/negative per-rule tests. More importantly, the T-030 JVM sidecars currently validate XML well-formedness and dependency/startup boundaries only; services/validator-rpc.md explicitly says full KoSIT/phive/Saxon domain rule invocation is downstream, so T-031 cannot meet its strict 99.9% parity gate today. Added blockers: invoices-7psv for real KoSIT/phive EN16931 rule-result sidecar output, invoices-nq4p for a checked 81-rule coverage/IR gap matrix, plus existing format blockers invoices-t-040-ubl-2-1-parser-serializer-1v2 and invoices-t-041-cii-parser-serializer-gyl so differential tests have real UBL/CII paths. I am marking T-031 blocked rather than landing a partial validator that would silently miss required rules.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Current blocker audit after ready-queue alignment: direct Beads now reports ready_issues=0 and br ready returns [], but bv/ntm still surface T-031/dependency-held work as actionable. Rechecked T-031 evidence on 2026-05-27: blockers invoices-7psv and invoices-nq4p are closed, but PR #122 remains draft+dirty and explicitly implements only the 34 currently testable BR/BR-CO rules while exposing 47 IR-gap rules as deferred metadata. T-031 still cannot meet strict acceptance: every BR/BR-CO typed Rust function, positive+negative per-rule tests, and 99.9% JVM differential parity over the conformance corpus. Do not claim/close from PR #122 without resolving the remaining IR-gap and differential-parity gates.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design update after closing invoices-pm89: formal dependencies are closed and KoSIT now emits native BR findings. The remaining immediate validator inventory gap is BR-CO-05..BR-CO-08. The checked source matrix shows these reason-code/reason equivalence rows have official Schematron assertions with test=true(), so they are reference-non-enforceable rather than raw-XML parser gaps. I will align the Rust inventory by making these four rules explicit reference-parity no-finding checks with tests/comments, then re-run the validate-ubl-cii targeted gates and leave true differential-corpus parity as the remaining T-031 gate if still missing.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: BR-CO inventory gap aligned. BR-CO-05..BR-CO-08 are now explicit reference-parity no-finding checks because the pinned CEN/KoSIT UBL and CII Schematron rows assert true(); coverage reports 81/81 implemented and 0 deferred. Added a regression test proving these rule IDs are in the implemented inventory and are not emitted for reason-code/reason fixtures. Local gates: cargo test -p invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii --all-targets; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii --all-targets -- -D warnings; RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc -p invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii --no-deps; cargo fmt --all --check; python3 -m pytest tools/release-checks/test_en16931_coverage.py -q; release-mode 1 MB validator perf test; git diff --check; UBS on lib.rs (0 critical; existing test unwrap/assert/perf heuristic warnings remain). Remaining T-031 gate before closure: real differential corpus parity against JVM KoSIT/phive over the conformance corpus.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: added a live differential parity harness in tools/validator-parity plus the invoicekit-en16931-findings Rust probe. The harness compares Rust BR/BR-CO findings against live JVM sidecar JSON-RPC responses and fails closed for missing sidecars, KoSIT configuration/library findings, or oracle precondition failures. Gates run: python3 -m pytest tools/validator-parity/tests/test_en16931_parity.py -q; cargo test -p invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii --all-targets; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check --package invoicekit-validate-ubl-cii. Live phive check after rebuilding invoicekit/validator-phive:ci: python3 tools/validator-parity/en16931_parity.py --phive-url http://127.0.0.1:32777 --limit 1 --timeout 30 exits 2 with PHIVE-UNNAMED [SAX] before any BR comparison because projected fixture conformance-corpus/synthetic/ubl-2-1/ubl-2-1-0001/fixture.xml has UBL schema-order/content issues. Opened blocker invoices-rz3l and made T-031 depend on it. T-031 remains open; 99.9% JVM parity is not proven yet.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Completion gate: live differential parity now passes against both JVM oracles on the committed UBL corpus. KoSIT sidecar image bundles pinned official XRechnung config v2026-01-31 and maps the actual KoSIT 1.6.2 reflection API; parity harness projects the official KoSIT customization URN and fills missing endpoint scheme IDs before normalization. Verified: combined KoSIT+phive run over 50 fixtures each, 0 mismatches, parity 1.0 for both backends.

title: Validation result schema (rule ID, severity, BT/BG term, location, fix, citation)

## Goal

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] `ValidationResult` has fields: rule_id, severity, term, location, suggested_fix, citation, trace?.

ValidationResult struct (rule_id/severity/term/location/suggested_fix/citation/optional trace) + schemars-derived JSON Schema artifact at schemas/validation-result.schema.json + CI gate (tools/release-checks/test_schemas_match.py) that re-derives the schema and diffs against the committed copy. 10 unit + 11 doctest + 1 proptest. 11 CI contexts green. clippy.toml extended with KoSIT/phive/Saxon/VeriFactu/XPath/XSLT/veraPDF/JSON-Patch. PR admin-merged because both agents share GitHub identity. Waivers: backend-specific producers (rust-native, jvm:*, rest:official, partner, cli:*, none) wired up by their owning beads — this bead ships the contract, not the producers.

title: Validator explain-plan trace
description:
## Background

Idea-wizard top-2 pick. For any validation result, emit a structured trace of every rule evaluated in order with {rule_id, evaluated_at_path, inputs, decision, citations}. Both machine-readable JSON and human-readable Markdown.

## Goal

`invoicekit validate file.xml --explain` produces an explain-plan trace.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Trace shape documented; JSON Schema committed.
- [ ] Markdown rendering produces a readable narrative.
- [ ] Snapshot tests on at least 5 sample invoices.

- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.

1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### Validator gates (label `validators`)
- [ ] Differential parity vs the named JVM oracle: ≥ 99.9% rule-result match on the conformance corpus. Mismatches are catalogued, classified (oracle bug vs. our bug), and tracked.
- [ ] Per-rule unit tests: at least one positive and one negative case per rule.

Powers the language server hover, the docs site, and the customer support tooling.

## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §6 Track 2 T-032a.

title: Browser/edge validator capability matrix
description:
## Background

WebAssembly builds can include serializers but not necessarily validators that need external backends. The capability matrix makes this explicit at runtime: `serialize`, `local_validate`, `reference_validate`, `requires_service`, `requires_cli`, `unavailable_in_wasm`. External backends return `RequiresExternalBackend`, never panic.

## Goal

A per-country/profile/date capability matrix reachable via API + CLI; WASM validators return typed errors when an external backend is required.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Matrix data model documented and tested.
- [ ] `RequiresExternalBackend` error type implemented and returned everywhere.
- [ ] WASM build never silently downgrades or panics.
- [ ] CLI: `invoicekit capabilities --runtime=wasm` reports correctly.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:

3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### WebAssembly gates (label `wasm`)
- [ ] WASM artifact size measured and tracked; bundle stays under the budget documented in PLAN.md §2.1 (default 5 MB with feature set `de,fr,it,peppol`).
- [ ] Feature flags compile cleanly: at least 3 distinct feature combinations build and pass tests.
- [ ] Cold-start latency on Cloudflare Workers under the budget in PLAN.md (best-effort; benchmark recorded if exceeded).
- [ ] External-backend calls (`jvm:*`, `cli:*`, `rest:official`, `partner`) return `RequiresExternalBackend` errors with remediation text — they never panic, never silently downgrade.

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

PLAN.md §2.1, §4.5.
close_reason: Closed direct on main per principal pivot away from PR branches: runtime-aware capability matrix now reports native/wasm support, RequiresExternalBackend diagnostics, and nonlocal WASM operations; WASM ABI external validator requests return typed requires_external_backend errors. Fresh-eyes blockers fixed before close. Gates: cargo test -p invoicekit-cli -p invoicekit-wasm --all-targets --locked; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-cli -p invoicekit-wasm --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings; cargo doc/doc-tests with -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; wasm32 checks for default, country-de+format-peppol, and full features; capabilities schema pytest; TypeScript generator/check/tests; CLI wasm smoke; license header; git diff --check; UBS critical=0. Waived/N/A: PR approval/merge gate replaced by direct-main Agent Mail workflow at user instruction; Cloudflare cold-start and artifact-size measurement not changed by this matrix/error-surface patch; no rendering/perf/fuzz surface touched.

comments.author: PeachCedar
comments.text: Design: Extend the existing capabilities matrix and CLI in place rather than adding a new planner. Add explicit runtime metadata for each profile, a typed RequiresExternalBackend diagnostic, and a --runtime=native|wasm resolver path that reports external JVM/CLI/service requirements instead of silently downgrading. Wire schema and CI smoke checks, then expose the same typed external-backend diagnostic from the wasm crate for browser callers.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: runtime-aware capability matrix implementation is complete locally. Local gates passed: capabilities matrix pytest, TypeScript generator/check/round-trip, invoicekit-cli + invoicekit-wasm tests, clippy -D warnings, rustdoc/doc-tests, fmt check, wasm32 default/country-de+format-peppol/full feature checks, CLI --runtime=wasm smoke, license-header check, git diff --check, and UBS with Critical=0. Fresh-eyes request sent via Agent Mail (#1452) before close.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up: addressed the review blockers before close. WASM process_engine_abi_json now intercepts external validator ABI operations and returns requires_external_backend instead of unsupported_operation; unavailable_in_runtime is derived from capability levels and parse_matrix rejects unavailable_in_wasm drift; Rust parsing no longer accepts schema-required capability arrays as serde defaults. Re-ran affected gates after fixes.

title: Time-travel validation (date-pinned rule packs)

title: Public free validator web UI (dual mode)
description:
## Background

Free public validator at `validate.invoicekit.org`. Two explicit modes: local browser-only (privacy-first) and server-assisted reference (official-parity). UI surfaces which mode produced the result.

## Goal

A public single-page web app for invoice validation, dual-mode, deployed to `validate.invoicekit.org`.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Local mode runs entirely in browser via WASM; nothing leaves the device.
- [ ] Reference mode calls the JVM sidecar service; clearly labeled, no-retention by default.
- [ ] UI shows mode + rule pack version + validator backend per result.
- [ ] Deployed and reachable at `validate.invoicekit.org`.
- [ ] Analytics: page visits + drop-off (no PII, no payload retention).

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

(No type-specific gates apply to this bead beyond the universal gates above.)

## Implementation notes

Use Next.js. Static export for the local-mode page; serverless function for reference-mode calls.

## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §3.3 Phase 7 conformance and trust infrastructure.

title: Universal Business Language 2.1 parser and serializer
description:
## Background

UBL 2.1 is the European invoice XML standard underlying Peppol BIS, XRechnung, and many national variants. Parser and serializer live in `crates/format-ubl`.

## Goal

A round-trip-stable UBL 2.1 parser and serializer for Invoice + CreditNote document types.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Parser handles all UBL 2.1 Invoice and CreditNote elements per OASIS UBL 2.1 specification.
- [ ] Serializer emits canonical UBL XML; output passes T-019 canonicalization byte-identically across runs.
- [ ] Round-trip property test: parse → serialize → parse produces equal IRs for 50 fixture invoices.
- [ ] Schema validation passes against OASIS XSD for all serialized outputs.
- [ ] Performance: parse 1 MB invoice under 100 ms (p95) on a baseline reference machine.

close_reason: Implemented in PR #87: preserved/replayed non-core UBL Invoice/CreditNote top-level fields via document-fields extension, removed UnsupportedGap rows, added replay validation and regression tests; Russell fresh-eyes review returned NO BLOCKERS; final local workspace test/clippy passed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: create crates/format-ubl with a quick-xml streaming parser and deterministic serializer for the current invoicekit-ir CommercialDocument surface (Invoice and CreditNote). The serializer emits UBL 2.1 namespace-qualified XML and canonicalizes through invoicekit-canonical so two runs are byte-identical; the parser accepts UBL Invoice/CreditNote roots, extracts the IR fields that exist today, and reports typed errors for malformed XML, unsupported document roots, missing required IR fields, invalid numbers/dates/currency, and unsupported line structures. Tests will include happy-path Invoice and CreditNote round-trips, at least three failure modes, a 50-fixture generated round-trip suite, canonicalization idempotence, and a 1 MiB parse benchmark wired into the existing perf budget. Blocker note: the current IR cannot semantically represent every OASIS UBL 2.1 Invoice/CreditNote element, and no OASIS XSD corpus is vendored yet; I will not close T-040 until those strict gates are either implemented or explicitly split/waived.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: scoped UBL crate implementation is in place but T-040 remains not closable against the strict gates. Shipped locally so far: new invoicekit-format-ubl workspace crate, quick-xml parser for Invoice/CreditNote roots into current CommercialDocument fields, canonical serializer through invoicekit-canonical, typed errors, 50 generated round-trip fixtures, property test for parse/serialize stability, and ubl-parse Criterion bench wired to the perf budget. Verification green: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p invoicekit-format-ubl; cargo test -p invoicekit-bench-harness --benches; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-format-ubl --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo bench -p invoicekit-bench-harness --bench ubl_parse -- --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --sample-size 20 (5.2854-5.4819 ms); cargo test --workspace; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps. Remaining blockers for closing strict T-040: full semantic coverage of all OASIS UBL 2.1 Invoice/CreditNote elements is not representable in the current IR; no vendored OASIS XSD corpus or schema validation harness exists.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Review: draft PR #15 opened for scoped T-040 implementation: https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/15. Requested RainyBeaver fresh-eyes review via Agent Mail. T-040 remains in_progress and not closable because full OASIS UBL 2.1 element coverage plus XSD validation are still blocked by follow-ups invoices-pz5j, invoices-1ntf, and invoices-bbqm.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CI fix: PR #15 bench job failed because bench.yml did not run the new ubl_parse Criterion target while budget.toml tracked ubl-parse. Added ubl_parse to both pull_request bench and main publish regeneration paths. Local budget check with /tmp/cargo-target/criterion now reports ir-round-trip, xml-canonicalization, and ubl-parse all present.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Merged scoped progress PR #15: https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/15 (merge commit ac7014dcdd4b3e4b0aae5eb4b9bf91c4614a36ca). RainyBeaver fresh-eyes review id 1220: NO BLOCKERS for the scoped current-IR UBL parser/serializer, but T-040 remains not closed. Status moved to blocked and explicit dependencies added on invoices-pz5j, invoices-1ntf, and invoices-bbqm. Strict acceptance gaps still open: all OASIS UBL 2.1 Invoice/CreditNote element coverage, OASIS XSD schema validation for serialized outputs, and a true 50-fixture conformance corpus beyond generated same-shape round trips.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: final T-040 pass after dependencies closed. Remove the remaining silent-loss/unsupported top-level UBL gaps by preserving every non-core OASIS Invoice/CreditNote top-level element through the UBL document-fields JurisdictionExtension, while keeping core IR mappings deterministic and schema-valid. Update the coverage matrix so no row remains UnsupportedGap, add focused tests that inject representative simple and aggregate top-level fields and verify parse -> serialize -> parse preservation, then run the committed 50-fixture corpus, OASIS schema harness, release checks, clippy/test/doc, and fresh-eyes review before closing.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Final verification for T-040 after dependency closure and fresh-eyes review. Implemented preservation/replay for non-core OASIS UBL Invoice/CreditNote top-level elements through the existing urn:invoicekit:ubl:2.1:document-fields extension; coverage rows no longer use UnsupportedGap. Fresh-eyes review by Russell found two replay-validation blockers; both fixed. Final Russell result: NO BLOCKERS. Passing gates: python3 tools/conformance-corpus/validate_fixture_metadata.py; python3 -m pytest tools/release-checks/test_ubl_conformance_corpus.py -q; cargo test -p invoicekit-format-ubl --all-targets; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-format-ubl --all-targets -- -D warnings; RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc -p invoicekit-format-ubl --no-deps; cargo test --workspace --locked after first replay fix; cargo build --workspace --all-targets --locked; git diff --check; ubs crates/format-ubl/src/lib.rs crates/format-ubl/src/mapping.rs. UBL parse benchmark from this pass stayed under the 100ms gate at 9.24-9.54ms.

title: Cross Industry Invoice (CII) parser and serializer

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: create crates/format-cii following the format-ubl pattern, but for UN/CEFACT CrossIndustryInvoice D16B/100 syntax. The crate will expose to_xml(&CommercialDocument) and from_xml(&str), use quick-xml streaming parse, serialize a deterministic current-IR CII projection, canonicalize through invoicekit-canonical, and return typed CiiError variants for malformed XML, unsupported roots/type codes, missing required IR fields, invalid dates/decimals, and canonicalization failures. Tests will cover Invoice/CreditNote-equivalent type codes where representable, canonical byte stability, 50 generated CII round-trip fixtures, parser stability proptest, and failure modes. I will wire a 1 MiB cii-parse Criterion bench into the existing perf budget. Scope note: current invoicekit-ir cannot semantically represent the full CII D16B namespace, so I will file/attach explicit blockers for full element coverage and a real CII conformance corpus rather than closing T-041 on scoped progress alone.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: scoped current-IR CII crate is implemented locally. Added invoicekit-format-cii workspace member with quick-xml streaming parser, deterministic CII serializer through invoicekit-canonical, typed CiiError variants, 50 generated round-trip fixtures, parser stability proptest, doctests, and a cii-parse 1 MiB Criterion bench wired into bench.yml and tools/perf-budget/budget.toml. Focused verification green so far: cargo test -p invoicekit-format-cii (12 unit + 3 doctests), cargo clippy -p invoicekit-format-cii --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p invoicekit-bench-harness --benches, RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc -p invoicekit-format-cii --no-deps, pytest tools/perf-budget/tests -q, and cargo bench -p invoicekit-bench-harness --bench cii_parse measured about 3.04 ms for a 1 MiB CII invoice. Strict T-041 close remains blocked by full D16B coverage and real conformance corpus; added blockers invoices-rsho and invoices-h4b3.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Ready for fresh-eyes review: PR #26 opened at https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/26 with scoped current-IR CII parser/serializer progress. comments.author: durakovic

Strict T-041 close remains blocked by invoices-rsho and invoices-h4b3; PR #26 remains scoped progress only.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress after second fresh-eyes blocker: fixed invalid numeric XML character references by validating parsed codepoints against XML character ranges before resolving them into text. comments.text: Design: final T-041 pass after invoices-rsho and invoices-h4b3 closed. 1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.

- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

title: Factur-X / ZUGFeRD all six profiles
description:
## Background

Factur-X/ZUGFeRD has six profiles: MINIMUM, BASIC WL, BASIC, EN 16931, EXTENDED, XRECHNUNG. Each is a subset/superset of EN 16931 in CII form.

## Goal

All six profiles serialize and validate correctly.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Each of the six profiles has at least one valid and one invalid fixture.
- [ ] Profile downgrade and upgrade conversions (e.g. EN 16931 → BASIC) emit a populated LossinessLedger.
- [ ] Validation per profile against the official ZUGFeRD validator (T-052 veraPDF setup helps here).

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

close_reason: Closed by PR #92 — profile-xrechnung crate with to_xrechnung_3_x_xml + Leitweg-ID validation + 22-row BR-DE-* coverage matrix. 10 tests pass (5 unit + 3 coverage + 2 integration projecting 30 fixtures through CIUS-DE). title: T-046 follow-up: extend format-* from_xml to return (Doc, LossinessLedger)
description: Extend invoicekit_format_ubl::from_xml and invoicekit_format_cii::from_xml to return (CommercialDocument, LossinessLedger) so callers get per-fixture expected ledgers without round-tripping through compute_ledger. Updates the T-021a corpus tests to assert the ledger inline.
close_reason: PR #132 merged as cb6039b. UBL/CII from_xml now returns (CommercialDocument, LossinessLedger); parser ledger compares parse -> serialize -> parse via IR roundtrip comparison over the full top-level CommercialDocument surface. Fresh-eyes blockers were fixed before merge; CI and focused local gates passed. Caveat: this proves same-format parsed-IR roundtrip stability, not raw source-XML coverage for fields with no IR representation.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: Change the public format-ubl::from_xml and format-cii::from_xml APIs to return (CommercialDocument, LossinessLedger) and migrate Rust callers rather than adding compatibility shims. To avoid a dependency cycle, put the shared round-trip ledger comparison on the IR/lossiness type boundary and have the format parsers produce the inline parse ledger from the parsed document without calling invoicekit-lossiness-ledger-generator. Then update the UBL/CII corpus tests to assert ledger.lost is empty and the expected preserved paths are present for every committed fixture, while keeping the existing parse -> serialize -> parse equality and byte-stability checks.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: tuple API migration implemented. `format-ubl::from_xml` and `format-cii::from_xml` now return `(CommercialDocument, LossinessLedger)`, the shared field comparison lives on `LossinessLedger`, the lossiness-ledger-generator no longer carries a private duplicate `diff_ledger`, and UBL/CII corpus tests assert inline parser ledgers directly. Gates green so far: cargo fmt --all --check; focused cargo check --all-targets; focused cargo test --all-targets; clippy -D warnings; rustdoc -D warnings; git diff --check; UBS exit 0 with zero critical findings. Fresh-eyes review is running before PR.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes fixes: reviewer flagged the old 8-path comparison and self-compare parser ledger as PR blockers. Fixed by expanding `LossinessLedger::from_roundtrip_comparison` to the full 21-path CommercialDocument surface, removing the self-compare `from_parsed_document` helper, and computing parser-returned ledgers from parse -> serialize -> internal document-only reparse. Updated UBL/CII/lossiness corpus expected preserved path sets to 21. Re-ran focused check/test/clippy/doc/diff/UBS; UBS remains exit 0 with 0 critical findings. Fresh-eyes re-check requested.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes re-check cleared the two high blockers. Reviewer confirmed the comparison now covers all 21 top-level CommercialDocument paths and parser-returned ledgers now come from parse -> serialize -> internal reparse rather than self-compare. Residual caveat, explicitly accepted for this bead: this proves same-format parsed-IR roundtrip stability, not raw source-XML coverage for fields with no IR representation. Short Criterion samples after the semantic fix: ubl-parse 47.333-50.292 ms, cii-parse 29.151-30.631 ms with the new ledger-returning API.

title: Format auto-detection (sniff input bytes, return format identifier)
description:
## Background

Customers will hand us bytes and ask 'what is this?'. Format auto-detection sniffs the first N bytes and namespace declarations to identify the format.

## Goal

A `detect_format(bytes) -> FormatId` function with at least UBL, CII, Factur-X PDF, FatturaPA, KSeF FA(3), CFDI 4.0, ZUGFeRD profiles.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] At least 10 formats detected correctly.
- [ ] False-positive rate under 1% on the test corpus.
- [ ] Returns `Unknown` for unrecognized inputs (never panics).

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

close_reason: Completed by PR #18 (merge commit 4231064337e4177b9ad8ba9b89c2ac7a937cb126): deterministic Typst crate dependency, built-in smoke PDF renderer, cargo-audit/cargo-deny waiver config, and release-check path guard. Local and CI gates passed; fresh-eyes blockers fixed. T-052 veraPDF/PDF-A verification remains downstream by plan.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement T-050 inside crates/render-pdf with exact-pinned Typst 0.13.1 crates (latest compatible with the workspace rust-version 1.83; 0.14.2 requires Rust 1.89). Add an in-memory Typst World backed only by embedded Typst fonts via typst-kit embed-fonts with system fonts disabled, expose render_typst_pdf and render_hello_world_invoice, export PDF/A-3b bytes through typst-pdf with a fixed timestamp and stable identifier, and prove deterministic hello-world rendering with unit/doctests. The strict veraPDF validation gate is impossible until dependent T-052 ships; I will record that as an explicit waiver/blocker rather than pretending Typst internal PDF/A settings equal veraPDF validation.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: focused render-pdf gates are green locally (cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p invoicekit-render-pdf including doctests, cargo clippy -p invoicekit-render-pdf --all-targets -- -D warnings). Fresh-eyes pass found the expected strict-gate caveat: PLAN orders T-052 after T-050, so veraPDF validation must be recorded as a downstream waiver until the adapter exists.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Ready for review: PR #18 is open at https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/18. Local gates green: fmt, focused render-pdf test/clippy, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, rustdoc with -D warnings, cargo deny check, cargo audit with allowed Typst-transitive warnings, and ubs on touched files. Waiting on CI and RainyBeaver fresh-eyes review.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CI green on PR #18: rustfmt, clippy, ubuntu+macos build/test, rustdoc, cargo audit, cargo deny, SPDX header, perf-budget tooling tests, and bench pull_request all passed; GitHub Pages publish correctly skipped on PR. Waiting on RainyBeaver fresh-eyes review.

title: veraPDF adapter for conformance verification

## Goal

An adapter that calls veraPDF and parses its output.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] `validator-verapdf` sidecar deployed.

title: PDF/A-3 dictionary post-processing

title: Factur-X XML embedding into PDF/A-3 attachment
