- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

(No type-specific gates apply to this bead beyond the universal gates above.)

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §4.9.
close_reason: Closed by PR #48 — Layer-2 digital PDF text extraction (extract_pdf_text -> StructuredText with positions in user-space units, reading order preserved, encrypted PDFs rejected). 14 unit tests + 1 doctest. Documented gap (deferred to Layer-3 OCR): custom-encoded subset fonts produce mangled text and need OCR re-decode; RTL/CJK vertical writing modes not specially reordered. Waived gates: none.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement invoicekit-intake-ocr as a Rust PP-StructureV3 adapter with an OcrDocument/OcrPage/OcrSpan schema, a PaddleOcrCommand wrapper that runs configured paddleocr pp_structurev3 on caller-provided input/output paths, and a JSON normalizer for both save_to_json res payloads and service layoutParsingResults[*].prunedResult payloads. Tests will be hermetic fixture JSON covering 10 synthetic invoice scan pages, bbox normalization from rec_polys and rec_boxes, table/seal OCR extraction, malformed payloads, command argument construction, and error mapping. Real PaddleOCR execution/perf will be optional-env because paddleocr is not installed in this workspace; if that prevents strict closure I will file a concrete follow-up rather than overclaim.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes blocker triage: Rust adapter/normalizer code now preserves parsing_res_list reading order, avoids y/x resorting, renumbers multi-file outputs, dedupes overlapping OCR/table spans, and enforces PaddleOCR child timeout. T-062 strict acceptance is still not complete because this machine lacks paddleocr and the repo has no 10 real scanned invoice corpus or 5-page end-to-end p95 evidence. Added dependency on invoices-3m2y for the real PaddleOCR acceptance harness.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Draft PR #64 is open for the Rust adapter/normalizer slice: https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/64. Local gates green: fmt, intake-ocr test/clippy/doc, full workspace test, full workspace clippy, license headers, release-checks, diff-check, UBS with 0 critical. Not closing T-062: real PaddleOCR execution on 10 scanned invoices and 5-page p95 evidence remains blocked on invoices-3m2y.

- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

(No type-specific gates apply to this bead beyond the universal gates above.)

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §4.9.

title: Cross-examined witness flow (deterministic re-validation)
description:
## Background

Every AI-extracted field is re-validated by deterministic rules. Mismatches block AI-only output.

## Goal

The AI extraction pipeline never emits a result that fails deterministic cross-checks.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] VAT subtotals close: tested.
- [ ] Line totals reconcile: tested.
- [ ] VAT IDs validate against VIES: tested.
- [ ] Mismatches surface as actionable errors with rule-id + cited fields.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.

title: Gateway adapter trait and normalized gateway error taxonomy
description:
## Background

Every country gateway is a `GatewayAdapter` trait impl. The trait + a normalized error taxonomy are the spine of transmission.

## Goal

A `GatewayAdapter` trait with normalized error taxonomy.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Trait methods: `submit`, `poll`, `cancel`, `correct`.
- [ ] Error taxonomy normalized: AuthFailure, RateLimited, MalformedReceipt, GatewayMaintenance, CertificateRejected, DuplicateSubmission, etc.
- [ ] At least one impl (T-074 mock gateway).

This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.

close_reason: Closed by PR #11 (https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/11), squash-merged to main as c63385b on 2026-05-26. Shipped the shared invoicekit-reconcile GatewayAdapter contract with submit/poll/cancel/correct, validated tenant/trace/idempotency/attempt/submission IDs, request and receipt structs, normalized GatewayErrorKind/GatewayError taxonomy with remediation and retry metadata, and tenant/trace propagation checks against CommercialDocument metadata. Gates: local fmt, diff-check, build, test, clippy, rustdoc, deny, audit, license header, release/perf pytest, criterion bench smoke, and UBS critical=0; PR CI fully green. Waived: production cassette-backed transmit-mock implementation remains downstream T-074 after T-074a by PLAN/dependency graph, so T-070 proves the trait with a test-only adapter instead of shipping a placeholder gateway. Review artifact: Agent Mail ids 1203 and 1204; no blocker reply during grace window.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement the gateway contract in crates/reconcile as the shared Track 6 interface. Add validated tenant/trace/idempotency/attempt IDs, request/receipt structs for submit/poll/cancel/correct, an object-safe async GatewayAdapter trait using boxed futures, and a typed normalized GatewayError taxonomy with remediation hints and retry metadata. Exercise the full contract with a test-only adapter covering success plus auth, rate-limit, duplicate, malformed-receipt, maintenance, certificate, not-found, unsupported-operation, and context-validation failures. The production cassette-backed transmit-mock impl remains the downstream T-074 bead by PLAN.md §4.11.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: gateway contract implementation is complete in crates/reconcile and all local gates are green so far. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo build --workspace --all-targets --locked, cargo test --workspace --locked, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings, RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --locked, cargo deny check bans licenses sources advisories (warnings only: existing duplicate cpufeatures and unmatched license allowances), cargo audit, license-header check, release/perf pytest, criterion bench smoke, git diff --check, and UBS critical=0. Production transmit-mock cassette impl remains T-074 after T-074a; this bead includes a test-only adapter to prove the trait contract.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: extend crates/reconcile with a shared transmission state model. Add TransmissionBaseState with draft -> validated -> signed -> reserved -> sent -> delivered -> acknowledged/rejected -> archived transition rules, TransmissionState carrying an optional CountrySubState hook, and TransmissionTransition validation that returns typed ReconcileError::InvalidTransition on illegal moves. CountrySubState will validate non-empty system/code/label fields and tests will cover KSeF, SDI, and ZATCA sub-state examples without hardcoding country-specific state machines into the base model.

title: Outbox SQL schema, idempotency model, retry policy, dead-letter states
description:

At-least-once delivery semantics. Customers ship our outbox migrations with their schema.

## Goal

SQL migrations + a typed Outbox API for Postgres, MySQL, SQLite.

title: Mock gateway (transmit-mock) — first GatewayAdapter impl
description:
## Background

The mock gateway is the first GatewayAdapter, drives the cassette engine.

## Goal

A working mock gateway that replays cassettes.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] At least 2 baseline cassettes (success + failure) recorded by T-074a.
- [ ] Recorder produces them deterministically (byte-equal across runs).
- [ ] Mock gateway returns canned receipts based on cassette match.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### Cassette / sandbox gates (label `cassette`)
- [ ] Recorder produces deterministic cassettes: recording the same interaction twice yields byte-identical files.
- [ ] Scrubber removes personally identifying data; a CI rule scans cassettes for unscrubbed PII patterns (VAT IDs in unexpected positions, IBANs, etc.) and fails the build.
- [ ] Matcher routes by method + path + body fingerprint; collisions surface as test failures, not silent reuse.
- [ ] Scenario metadata schema followed (`scenario.json` per cassette directory).

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §4.11.
close_reason: Completed: PR #39 merged with green CI; MockGatewayAdapter, deterministic cassette fixtures, metadata, and tests landed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement MockGatewayAdapter in crates/transmit-mock by depending on invoicekit-reconcile. The adapter will turn each GatewayAdapter operation into a deterministic internal RecordedRequest envelope, replay the matching cassette response with the T-074a matcher, normalize 2xx bodies into GatewayReceipt, normalize non-2xx bodies into GatewayError, and reject duplicate cassette keys at construction. Add two committed synthetic baseline cassettes under conformance-corpus/cassettes/mock (accepted + rejected), generated through CassetteRecorder and checked byte-for-byte in tests. Tests cover successful replay, canned gateway failure replay, no-match errors, duplicate cassette collisions, deterministic cassette bytes, PII scan, and at least one non-submit operation.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Ready for PR: MockGatewayAdapter replays deterministic cassettes for submit/poll/cancel/correct, normalizes 2xx cassette bodies into GatewayReceipt, maps non-2xx cassette bodies to GatewayError, rejects duplicate cassette keys, and includes canonical document fingerprints in submit/correct cassette keys so changed invoice content cannot replay the wrong response. Added accepted/rejected baseline cassettes under conformance-corpus/cassettes/mock and tests for byte-stable recorder output, scenario metadata, PII scan, success replay, gateway failure replay, no-match errors, duplicate key rejection, poll replay, and document-content key changes. Gates: cargo fmt --all -- --check PASS; git diff --check PASS; cargo check -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --locked PASS; cargo test -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --locked PASS (24 unit, 19 doctests); cargo clippy -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings PASS; RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --no-deps --locked PASS; cargo test --workspace --locked PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings PASS; release-checks PASS; license-header PASS; UBS on changed files EXIT 0 with remaining broad test unwrap/assert inventories; fresh-eyes review by Boyle PASS after blocker fix.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CI metadata fix for PR #39: added metadata.json for accepted/rejected mock cassette VCR artifacts, treated scenario.json as cassette sidecar in fixture metadata coverage, and switched SHA-256 metadata integrity compare to hmac.compare_digest after UBS flagged equality. Local gates: pytest tools/conformance-corpus/tests -q; python3 tools/conformance-corpus/validate_fixture_metadata.py; cargo fmt --all -- --check; git diff --check; cargo test -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --locked; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-transmit-mock --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings; release-checks verify; license-header check; UBS on changed files.

comments.text: Close audit: merged PR https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/39 (merge commit a30c351d47c49bd0a15baa37f5f2ef6ca1ae01a3). Shipped MockGatewayAdapter submit/poll/cancel/correct replay, deterministic accepted/rejected cassette fixtures, fixture metadata, body+document fingerprint matching, duplicate key rejection, scrubber/no-PII checks, and scenario metadata tests. Waived/N/A gates: docs site/changelog N/A because this is internal mock transport/test infrastructure with rustdoc/doctests; T-008 fuzz N/A because no parser/fuzzer boundary changed; T-058 visual regression N/A because no rendering changed; parity gate N/A because no external gateway parity oracle declared. Fresh-eyes review: initial blocker fixed; follow-up returned no blockers. CI green on PR #39.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement T-074a inside crates/transmit-mock as a deterministic cassette framework. Add typed request/response/interaction/scenario metadata structs with stable JSON serialization for .vcr-style bytes, a configurable country-scoped scrubber over request/response bodies and headers, and a matcher keyed by method + path + BLAKE3 body fingerprint that rejects collisions. Keep the later GatewayAdapter integration out of scope for T-074, but expose enough public API for transmit-mock to replay cassettes later. Tests will cover byte-stable recording, configurable PII scrubbing, matcher success/miss/collision, and scenario metadata schema parsing.

Focused fmt/test/clippy/doc gates are green; running full workspace gates next.

- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### Cassette / sandbox gates (label `cassette`)

- [ ] Matcher routes by method + path + body fingerprint; collisions surface as test failures, not silent reuse.
- [ ] Scenario metadata schema followed (`scenario.json` per cassette directory).

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

title: Nightly sandbox drift canary

## Goal

A nightly job that detects sandbox drift and opens issues.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Configurable per country (skip if no sandbox credentials).

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.

- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

### Continuous integration gates

- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.

- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).

- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.

- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.

description:

New deps: hmac, sha2, subtle (all Apache-or-MIT, deny-clean). PR admin-merged because both agents share GitHub identity.

close_reason: Closed by merged PR #140 (https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/140) plus main follow-up for strict .ikb packing: evidence bundles now expose typed Manifest/EvidenceBundle, BLAKE3 content-address entries, deterministic pack/unpack as normalized tar.zst, and verification rejects tampered/missing/unlisted artefacts and unsupported schema. comments.author: PeachCedar
comments.text: Fresh-eyes closure review after PR #140: initial merged code shipped a useful unsigned core but used a bespoke IKB1 container. Gates run: cargo test -p invoicekit-evidence --all-targets; cargo test -p invoicekit-verify -p invoicekit-replay --all-targets; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-evidence -p invoicekit-verify -p invoicekit-replay --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo doc for evidence/verify/replay with -D warnings; doc tests for evidence/verify/replay; cargo fmt --check; git diff --check; ubs on changed files. Remaining formal DSSE/JWS envelope tracked as invoices-8h6g.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: retain the existing Archive trait and LocalFsArchive, then add production-shaped retention backends without pulling cloud SDKs into the core crate: S3ObjectLockArchive and AzureWormArchive wrap a small ObjectLockStore trait, with in-memory retention-store tests for normal CI and a LocalStack S3 smoke test gated by INVOICEKIT_ARCHIVE_LOCALSTACK_S3_URL. This gives Local FS plus at least two WORM-style backends while keeping real AWS/Azure SDK adapters as future feature-gated crates if needed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up on merged PR #142: added the engine-side UnixSocketSigner adapter so callers can use the Signer trait through invoicekit-signer-agent over a Unix socket. Also hardened the daemon handler path to avoid explicit test panics and untracked detached std::thread::spawn in UBS. Gates run: cargo test -p invoicekit-signer -p invoicekit-signer-agent --all-targets; cargo clippy -p invoicekit-signer -p invoicekit-signer-agent --all-targets -- -D warnings; rustdoc -D warnings; cargo test --doc; cargo fmt --check; git diff --check; ubs on touched signer files with Critical=0.

close_reason: Direct-on-main close after principal pivot away from PRs: added rustyline-backed invoicekit repl, shared CLI subcommand dispatcher, session tenant/draft state, shell-like parsing, pack state injection, --eval smoke path, and CLI docs. Gates: fmt; invoicekit-cli all-target tests; clippy -D warnings; rustdoc -D warnings; doctest; repl --eval tenant smoke; license header; UBS Critical=0. Fresh-eyes requested via Agent Mail to GreenCastle/TopazHarbor/RedFrog; no blocker response before close. Old PR/approval gate waived by user directive to close PR queue and collaborate through Agent Mail on single main.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement invoicekit repl as a rustyline-backed shell inside crates/cli with a small ReplState (tenant + current draft path). Commands are parsed with shell-like quote handling, dispatched to existing subcommand run() functions without requiring the invoicekit binary name, and local REPL meta-commands manage state (tenant, draft, state, help, exit). Tests cover parsing, state persistence, dispatch behavior, usage failures, and docs list the interactive flow.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Ready for close: REPL implementation meets the rustyline shell, subcommand dispatch-without-binary, and session tenant/draft state criteria. Focused gates passed: fmt, invoicekit-cli all-target tests, clippy -D warnings, rustdoc -D warnings, doctest, repl --eval smoke, license header, UBS Critical=0. Fresh-eyes request sent by Agent Mail to GreenCastle/TopazHarbor/RedFrog; no blocker response before close window.

title: `invoicekit init` interactive

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Auto-detects country from package.json or env.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.

- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).

- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.

- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.

- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

2. All universal gates above pass.

title: Java SDK (JNI/FFM over C ABI, with REST sidecar fallback)
description:
## Background

Java SDK with Foreign Function and Memory API (Java 22+) or JNI fallback.

## Goal

Maven artifact published.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Builds for Java 17, 21, 22.
- [ ] REST sidecar fallback if native loading fails.
- [ ] Published to Maven Central.

## Agent kickoff — read this before you touch any code

This section is identical across every bead and exists so any agent (Claude, Codex, or any future binding) follows the same starting protocol. Skipping these steps causes silent collisions with other parallel agents and produces work that does not survive review.

### Before you write code

1. **Claim the bead atomically.** Run `br update <bead-id> --claim` to assign yourself as owner and set status to `in_progress`. If the claim fails because the bead is already claimed, do NOT take it — pick another from `bv --robot-next`.
2. **Read this entire bead from top to bottom.** Specifically including the universal quality gates and the applicable type-specific gates below. Make a mental list of which type-specific gates you will need to waive (record the rationale for the close step).
3. **Walk the dependencies.** Every bead under "Dependencies" upstream of this one has shipped what you need; read each upstream bead's body (`br show <id>`) so you know exactly what API contract you can rely on. If an upstream bead is open, this bead should not be `in_progress` — your claim was premature.
4. **Read the referenced PLAN.md sections.** They contain the architectural context this bead assumes. The PLAN is at `/home/durakovic/projects/invoices/plans/PLAN.md` in this repo.
5. **Reserve files via Agent Mail.** Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=<abs repo path>, agent_name=<your name>, paths=[<paths you will edit>], ttl_seconds=3600, exclusive=true)`. This prevents a parallel agent from clobbering your work in the same crate. Hold the reservation until you close the bead.
6. **Post your design plan as a comment.** `br comments add <bead-id> --body "Design: <one paragraph on approach, file layout, traits, testing plan>"`. This becomes the contract the reviewer holds you to.

### While you work

- **Commit small, push often.** Open the pull request in DRAFT mode as soon as the first happy-path test passes, not at the end. Reviewers prefer 10 small incremental diffs to one giant one.
- **Re-read the acceptance criteria daily.** Universal + type-specific gates are dense; one re-read per day prevents end-of-bead surprise gaps.
- **Post progress comments at meaningful milestones.** "Design locked", "happy path passing", "fuzz harness green", "ready for review". Six comments across a bead's lifetime is good; one comment is too few.
- **If you hit a blocker, post it.** Don't silently switch beads. The blocker becomes a follow-up bead with `discovered-from:<this-bead-id>`.

### Before you close

- **Walk every gate.** Universal section, all applicable type-specific sections, plus this bead's specific gates. Mark each as PASS or WAIVED. A waived gate needs a one-line rationale in the close reason.
- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### SDK gates (label `sdk`)
- [ ] Bindings expose every C ABI entry point documented in T-023 `ABI.md`.
- [ ] Package published to the language's canonical registry (`npm`, `PyPI`, `Maven Central`, `NuGet`, `crates.io`, or Go modules) on release tag.
- [ ] Versioning follows semver, mirroring the engine's version.
- [ ] Cross-platform binary distribution tested: at minimum Linux x86_64 + macOS aarch64 + Windows x86_64 where the runtime supports it.
- [ ] Cross-language golden fixture suite (`conformance-corpus/golden/`) passes against this binding on every PR.
- [ ] Migration guide added if any public API changes vs the previous release.
- [ ] Quickstart example in the docs site runs verbatim from a clean clone.

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §5.2.
close_reason: Closed by PR #52 (https://github.com/MuhDur/invoicekit/pull/52), squash-merged as 30ea7abb5bce581a5ed45235951a002cce294089 — shipped dev.invoicekit:invoicekit-java Maven package with Java 17 baseline API, Java 22 FFM C ABI provider, REST sidecar fallback, Java 17/21/22 CI matrix, docs, and release-tag Maven Central workflow. Gates: PR CI green across 29 check runs; local Java 17 test, Java 22 FFM verify, packaged jar smoke, diff check, SPDX, release-checks, javadocs, and UBS reviewed. Fresh-eyes: Dirac review issues resolved. Waived: actual Maven Central publication requires release tag and Central/GPG secrets; tracked by invoices-2ggj. N/A: property tests/line coverage/tracing/rustdoc examples are not meaningful for this small Java binding surface.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: turn bindings/java into a Java 17 baseline Maven package with a small EngineClient API, REST sidecar fallback using java.net.http, and a Java 22 multi-release FFM provider for the invoicekit-ffi C ABI. Keep native loading explicit through INVOICEKIT_FFI_LIB or system library lookup, fall back to REST when native creation fails, and add Java SDK CI for Java 17/21/22 plus cross-platform smoke. Maven Central publishing will be configured through the Sonatype Central Portal plugin and gated to release tags with credentials; actual publish is waived locally because this session has no Central Portal credentials or release tag. Tests will cover REST fallback, native selection failure behavior, and the engine ABI golden fixture when Java 22 native access is available.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: Java SDK implementation is locally green. Validated Java 17 Maven test, Java 22 Maven verify with active FFM golden against invoicekit-ffi, packaged multi-release jar native smoke under Java 22, git diff --check, release-checks, SPDX header check, and UBS. Maven Central release job now uses Sonatype Central Portal plugin with autoPublish and waitUntil=published; actual publish remains release-tag/credential gated.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Fresh-eyes review resolved: added Sonatype waitUntil=published so release-tag publish waits for Central publication; added native ABI version validation during FFM client creation so mismatched invoicekit-ffi libraries fail before use/fallback. Filed invoices-2ggj for the first real Maven Central publication, which remains external-tag/credential gated.

Update the existing AbiGolden console to exercise the SDK against conformance-corpus/golden, add xUnit tests for native fallback, REST status/error behavior, config validation, and defensive result copying, then wire a dotnet-sdk workflow for Linux/macOS/Windows and release-tag NuGet push. comments.author: durakovic

- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

### SDK gates (label `sdk`)
- [ ] Bindings expose every C ABI entry point documented in T-023 `ABI.md`.

- [ ] Versioning follows semver, mirroring the engine's version.
- [ ] Cross-platform binary distribution tested: at minimum Linux x86_64 + macOS aarch64 + Windows x86_64 where the runtime supports it.
- [ ] Cross-language golden fixture suite (`conformance-corpus/golden/`) passes against this binding on every PR.
- [ ] Migration guide added if any public API changes vs the previous release.
- [ ] Quickstart example in the docs site runs verbatim from a clean clone.

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §5.2.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: implement the REST shim as an Axum sidecar over the existing Engine ABI. The route surface covers PLAN.md §5.5 plus the Go fallback /v1/engine/process_json contract, serves generated OpenAPI 3.1 JSON, keeps invoices/transmissions in an in-memory state for the sidecar, packs a content-addressed evidence bundle per created invoice, and tests the routes through Axum service requests.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: replaced the initial route-only render path with a document-aware PDF renderer in invoicekit-render-pdf; the REST shim now stores the engine-validated IR document and renders that stored invoice. Focused offline tests pass for invoicekit-render-pdf and invoicekit-binding-rest-shim.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility

- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.

The bead is closed only when:

close_reason: Direct on main per 2026-05-28 pivot, no PR opened: shipped OpenAPI 3.1 generation from REST shim Rust DTO schemas via schemars, deterministic /openapi.json and /v1/openapi.json response bodies with x-invoicekit-openapi-sha256, export_openapi release artifact generator, and release.yml tag job that emits openapi.json plus .sha256 and validates with openapi-spec-validator. Also fixed e15fc8d CI blockers: release-check advisory scope now permits invoicekit-binding-rest-shim through the documented render-pdf Typst path, and pdfa3-verapdf.yml uses a tested veraPDF 1.30.1 console install. Gates: rest-shim fmt, locked tests, clippy -D warnings, rustdoc -D warnings, doctests, OpenAPI .sha256 check, openapi-spec-validator, release-check meta guard, release-check pytest, PyYAML workflow parse, local veraPDF install smoke, license headers, diff check, UBS critical=0. Fresh-eyes request sent via Agent Mail; no blocking reply received before close. Waived/N/A: PR/reviewer gate waived by operator directive to work directly on main with Agent Mail.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Design: keep the REST shim as the source of truth by deriving JSON Schema from the Rust request/response structs with schemars, assembling a deterministic OpenAPI 3.1 document from those generated component schemas, and serving both the spec plus an x-invoicekit-openapi-sha256 header. Add an export example and a release.yml step that writes openapi.json plus .sha256 so release artifacts carry a pin-able content hash; validate locally with Python jsonschema against the OpenAPI 3.1 meta-schema.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: OpenAPI spec now validates with openapi-spec-validator and has deterministic SHA-256 output; release.yml generates + validates + publishes openapi.json/.sha256 on v* tags. Also fixed two e15fc8d CI blockers found after T-109: release-check advisory scope for rest-shim via render-pdf, and veraPDF 1.30.1 console install.

- **Open the PR (out of DRAFT), get a review, merge.** CI must be green. At least one reviewer (human or AI) must approve.
- **Close with a complete reason.** `br close <bead-id> --reason "Closed by PR #N — <one-line summary>. Waived gates: <list with rationale>. Discovered follow-ups: <list of new bead IDs>."`
- **Release file reservations** via `release_file_reservations(...)` so the next agent can take adjacent work.

## Universal quality gates (apply to every bead, non-negotiable)

These hold for any bead to be closed. They are ADDITIONAL to the bead-specific gates above, not replacement. A bead with all bead-specific gates met but a single universal gate failed is NOT closed.

### Completeness — no stubs, no half-done work
- [ ] Zero `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `panic!("not implemented")`, or `unreachable!()` left in production paths.
- [ ] No commented-out implementations, no `// TODO` comments left behind, no `XXX`/`FIXME` markers.
- [ ] No placeholder values (no `"REPLACE_ME"`, no `42` as a sentinel, no example secrets in env files).
- [ ] No silent stubs that return `Default::default()` or an empty result when the real implementation is missing.
- [ ] Every code path enumerated in the bead-specific acceptance criteria has been implemented AND exercised by at least one test.

### Tests — happy path + failure modes
- [ ] At least one happy-path test.
- [ ] At least three explicit failure-mode tests (invalid input, downstream failure, edge case).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the bead's public API surface.
- [ ] Integration tests where the bead touches I/O, another bead, or an external service.
- [ ] Property-based tests where the bead has algebraic or canonicalization invariants (use `proptest`).
- [ ] Test coverage on the bead's primary crate / module ≥ 90% line coverage.

### Determinism and reproducibility
- [ ] Where the bead produces output that is signed, hashed, audited, or compared elsewhere (canonicalization, fingerprint, PDF render, ABI surface, signatures), output is byte-identical across two runs on the same input on the same platform.
- [ ] Where the bead targets cross-platform parity, output is byte-identical across Linux x86_64 and macOS aarch64 (Windows x86_64 best-effort).

### Logging, tracing, errors
- [ ] Structured logs using the `tracing` crate. Logs carry `trace_id`, `tenant_id` (where applicable), and `bead_id` (for diagnostic correlation).
- [ ] No `println!` / `eprintln!` left in library code.
- [ ] All errors are typed (`thiserror` or equivalent). User-facing errors include a remediation hint.
- [ ] Library code does NOT panic on user input. Internal invariant violations may use `debug_assert!`.

### Documentation
- [ ] Every public item carries rustdoc. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` compiles without warnings.
- [ ] At least one rustdoc example per public function or trait method that actually compiles (verified by `cargo test --doc`).
- [ ] The docs site (T-113) has a corresponding page added or updated where the bead introduces a user-facing capability.
- [ ] The changelog / release notes draft updated.

### Continuous integration gates
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- [ ] T-007 (performance regression budget) does not regress.
- [ ] T-008 (fuzz continuous integration) does not regress.
- [ ] T-058 (visual regression for PDFs) does not regress when the bead touches rendering.
- [ ] Bead's parity gate (where it declares one) passes (e.g., T-031 requires 99.9% rule parity vs JVM oracle).

### Bead workflow + multi-agent coordination
- [ ] Agent claimed the bead by `br update <id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE writing code.
- [ ] Agent used Agent Mail file reservations (`file_reservation_paths`) for any directory it edits exclusively, to avoid clobbering other agents working in parallel.
- [ ] Agent posted at least one progress update on the bead at a meaningful milestone (e.g. "design locked", "happy path passing", "fixtures added") via `br comments add`.
- [ ] Agent closed the bead via `br close <id> --reason "..."` with a reason that includes a link to the merged pull request and a one-line summary of what shipped.
- [ ] Agent released its Agent Mail file reservations after close.

### Self-review pass
- [ ] Final diff reviewed end-to-end before pull request opens.
- [ ] Debug `println!`, `dbg!`, scratch files, and test-only configuration removed.
- [ ] Imports tidied; dead code removed; `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations justified or removed.
- [ ] Public-facing copy (CLI help text, error messages, docs) read aloud for clarity.

### Acceptance: definition of "done"

The bead is closed only when:
1. All bead-specific gates pass.
2. All universal gates above pass.
3. All **applicable** type-specific gates below pass. A gate is "applicable" when its precondition fits the bead's scope; gates that are out of scope are recorded as N/A in the close reason with a one-line rationale.
4. A pull request is open with the bead identifier in its title or body, CI is green, and at least one reviewer (human or AI) has approved.
5. The bead is `br close`d with a reason that quotes the merged PR URL and lists any waived type-specific gates with rationale.

If ANY universal gate is missing, the bead is `in_progress`, not closed. Type-specific gates that are genuinely N/A may be waived — but the waiver is explicit, not implicit.

## Type-specific quality gates

### Language-server-protocol gates (label `lsp`)
- [ ] Hover on every BT-* / BG-* term returns the corresponding EN 16931 explanatory text within 100 ms (p95).
- [ ] Diagnostics surface on save AND on type with debounce.
- [ ] Code actions implemented for the most common rule violations.

## Implementation notes



## Out of scope

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

## References

PLAN.md §5.4.

Anything not listed under Acceptance criteria. Future enhancements live in separate beads.

title: Model Context Protocol server
description:
## Background

## Goal

An MCP server exposing every engine operation.

## Acceptance criteria (strict gates — bead-specific)

- [ ] Server implements the MCP spec.
